experimental_comparison.control_principle: Use the same model and as close as possible to the same worker harness. Change only the execution-control layer.

core_suite.label: Simple coding baseline
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should not significantly improve short single-patch tasks; any large lift here may indicate harness confounding.

core_suite.label: Hard long-horizon repo issue repair
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should improve honest continuation, evidence tracking, and retry behavior on larger issue-repair tasks.

core_suite.label: Terminal and system work
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should help most when build/debug/setup work spans many terminal steps and failure states.

core_suite.label: Skill and procedure following
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should improve workflows that require explicit procedural state, reusable skills, and step evidence.

core_suite.label: Repository from scratch
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should improve multi-file repo generation through explicit decomposition, verification, and closure checks.

core_suite.label: Human-calibrated long work
core_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should primarily improve completion honesty, restartability, and progress reporting on 1h to 8h+ tasks.

extension_suite.label: Broad real-work hidden-reference tasks
extension_suite.paper_role: Diverse external oracle after adapter maturity
extension_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should help with diverse real-world artifact tasks, but infra cost and environment variance make this an extension suite.

extension_suite.label: Workplace multi-app coordination
extension_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should provide shared durable state when work crosses apps, agents, and communication surfaces.

extension_suite.label: Long-term coherence
extension_suite.hypothesis: TaskOps should reduce drift and premature closure over very long simulated horizons, though this is less code-centric.

reported_only_not_core.reason: Qwen3.6-27B reports strong scores, but public task definitions and harness details are not pinned enough for core paper evidence.
Useful extension for non-Python coding tasks, but public harness/task pinning should be confirmed before core use.

taskops_metrics.description: Benchmark-native score, pass rate, or task-specific artifact score.
Fraction of runs marked complete despite missing required artifacts, failed benchmark scoring, or missing EoW evidence.
Fraction of executed tasks with run node, source task reference, artifact reference, and EoW.
Fraction of intentionally interrupted runs that resume without duplicated claims or lost progress.
No duplicate active claims, max concurrency obeyed, stale leases handled.
Step-0 decomposition, wave reports, blockers, and final answer are reported to the master sink.
Wall time, token count, worker attempts, and retries compared with the direct-agent baseline.
